summary: Adversarially-verified audit of llm-bim against the AE-rendering / CD-sheet / full-MEP excellence bar
logs: Auditing 9 dimensions against real Schad artifacts at output/_audit/schad

result.grade: B- — an A-grade kernel (routing, sizing, IFC, viewer, SVG drafting all real and tested) shipping a C-grade flagship: all three north-star legs (CD sheets, renderings, full MEP) carry blocker- or high-severity gaps between the promise and the actual on-disk deliverable.

plan.executive_summary: llm-bim has an A-grade engineering substrate wrapped around a C-grade flagship deliverable. The kernel is real and tested: a Manhattan-A* MEP routing engine with elbow/riser/tee insertion, a Hazen-Williams/Darcy-Weisbach/Hunter's-curve sizing engine (27 passing unit tests), correct IFC4 with hosted openings that pass ifcopenshell boolean geometry, per-element glTF metadata driving an element-aware WebGL inspector, and genuinely professional SVG drafting (layered wall poche, multi-tier dimension strings, keynote system, defensible NEC/CPC/bearing calcs). The problem is that the flagship Schad CD does not exercise this substrate where it matters, so all three north-star legs miss the bar. (1) FULL MEP is delivered as ZERO routed MEP: every *_takeoff.json is [], the IFC has zero MEP entities, and the build never calls a single routing/sizing function — the hand-typed calc text, the plotted symbol, and the empty takeoff are three disconnected artifacts. (2) The printed CD set is unreadable and dishonest: opaque legend/keynote blocks live in scaled drawing space and bury the plan/elevations/sections under white boxes, the pure-Python PDF binder flattens every lineweight/dash/poche the SVGs correctly compute and silently drops path-based geometry, the written scale disagrees with the plotted scale, and the graphic bar prints metric on an imperial set. (3) The renderings leg is a live viewer only — no textured surfaces (zero UVs/textures), no glTF material extensions (glass is fake alpha, no baked lights), and no hero raster image anywhere in the pack. The fixes are highly leveraged because the hard engines already exist and pass tests; the work is wiring, rendering-fidelity, and a plot-set output rewrite, not new science.
plan.overall_grade: B- — an A-grade kernel (routing, sizing, IFC, viewer, SVG drafting all real and tested) shipping a C-grade flagship: all three north-star legs (CD sheets, renderings, full MEP) carry blocker- or high-severity gaps between the promise and the actual on-disk deliverable.

workstreams.name: Wire the routing + sizing engine into the flagship (deliver real MEP & trades)
workstreams.rationale: This is the single largest gap to the north star. 'FULL MEP and all trades' is currently delivered as literally zero routed MEP: duct/pipe/conduit/cable_tray/rebar/connections takeoffs are all [], trade_schedule rolls up 0 for every MEP trade, and model.ifc contains not one IfcPipeSegment/IfcDuctSegment/IfcFlowTerminal (histogram is architectural/structural only). Yet the capability is real and TESTED — mep_autoroute (A* with turn-weighted elbow minimization, wall-obstacle inflation, riser/tee insertion) and size_pipe/size_duct/wsfu_to_lps (Hazen-Williams / Darcy-Weisbach / Hunter's curve) pass 27 unit tests and populate takeoffs correctly for structural steel. The flagship build (projects/schad/build_llmbim.py) simply never calls them: MEP fixtures become create_note() text markers, equipment becomes size_assumed massing boxes, and schad_mep.py hand-types '3" building drain' / '3/4" service' strings that never touch mep_sizing. Fixing this simultaneously populates every empty takeoff, emits first-class IFC MEP entities for downstream clash/quantity, gives the MEP sheets real routed geometry to draw, and makes the calc numbers trace to one computed source.
sequenced_actions: In projects/schad/build_llmbim.py (and/or schad_mep.py), replace the note/massing MEP layer with kernel calls: for each modeled fixture run place_pipe/place_fitting for DWV + domestic-water + radiant PEX, place_duct for supply/return, place_conduit for feeders/branch circuits, and place sprinkler mains/branches — anchored to the fixture (x,y) coordinates schad_mep.py already computes.

Drive every size from mep_sizing instead of literals: wsfu_to_lps -> size_pipe for water, DFU -> drain size, CFM -> size_duct, and emit the calc strings (svg_plans.py plumbing_calc text) from those returned sizes so drawing size == takeoff size == calc number.

Add a conduit-fill module in mep_sizing.py (NEC Ch.9 Table 4/5 conductor-area vs raceway-area, 40% limit for 3+ conductors, 310.15 derate) analogous to size_pipe, and wire it into size_route by extending the kind whitelist (currently rejects anything but pipe/duct at mep_sizing.py:561).

Add rebar quantity derivation from the 17 IfcFooting + slab + stem-wall geometry (bar schedule by size/length/laps/weight from the '(2) #4 CONT' design basis in STRUCTURAL_CALCS.md) and code it CSI 03 20 00 so csi_takeoff spans concrete-reinforcing, not just Division 05.
Add IfcSystem grouping per trade in the IFC exporter so the now-routed IfcPipeSegment/IfcDuctSegment/IfcFlowTerminal entities carry system assignment for downstream clash-checking.
Add a drift-pin test asserting the flagship build produces non-empty duct/pipe/conduit/rebar takeoffs and >0 IFC MEP entities, so 'full MEP' can never silently regress to zero again.
findings: BLOCKER: Flagship Schad CD contains ZERO routed MEP — all *_takeoff.json are [] and trade_schedule rolls 0 for plumbing/fire/hvac/electrical/rebar; grep of projects/schad for place_pipe|place_duct|place_conduit|mep_autoroute finds no routing call (the engine is never invoked).

BLOCKER: model.ifc has no MEP whatsoever — zero IFCPIPESEGMENT/IFCDUCTSEGMENT/IFCFLOWTERMINAL/IFCSYSTEM etc.; histogram is IFCFOOTING/WALL/DOOR/WINDOW/COLUMN only, so the IFC cannot be clash-checked or quantified for any trade.

HIGH: schad_mep.py is a hand-authored data+string layer disconnected from the engine — electrical_devices() is hardcoded {sym,x,y,ckt} dicts, plumbing pipe sizes ('3" building drain','3/4" service') are typed literals, and the real, tested mep_sizing has zero callers in projects/.

HIGH: No conduit fill (NEC Ch.9) exists anywhere — mep_sizing has size_pipe/size_duct but no conduit-fill function; size_route rejects kind='conduit', so the electrical raceway trade is dimensioned by hand notes only despite the 'full electrical' claim.

MEDIUM: Rebar/concrete-reinforcement trade is named but unquantified for 17 footings + slab — rebar_takeoff.json is [], and csi_takeoff.json spans only Division 05 (structural steel), missing 03 20 00 concrete reinforcing.

STRENGTH to build on: mep_route.py (1185 lines, A* + elbow/riser/tee) and mep_sizing.py (721 lines, Hazen-Williams/Darcy-Weisbach/Hunter) are real and pass 27 unit tests; the takeoff pipeline already works end-to-end for structural steel (HSS6x6x1/4 x4, W16x40 x2 with CSI codes + element_ids), proving empty MEP takeoffs mean 'nothing routed', not 'function broken'.

workstreams.name: CD plot-set fidelity: legibility, lineweight hierarchy, scale honesty, pagination
workstreams.rationale: The printed PLOT_SET.pdf (the canonical stamped deliverable linked from index.html) fails at first glance in ways an AE reviewer rejects immediately. Two are blockers: opaque LINE LEGEND / KEYNOTES boxes are emitted inside scaled drawing space and balloon over the plan, both elevations, and the section — on A1.1 the keynote box maps to sheet-space 422x1664px and overruns the sheet entirely; and the pure-Python SVG->PDF binder reads zero stroke-widths, emits no line-width 'w' op, ignores dasharrays, hardcodes two lavender poche fills, and has no <path> handler — so it flattens the entire HEAVY/MEDIUM/LIGHT/DASHED hierarchy the SVGs correctly compute AND silently drops 31 path elements on the structural custom sheet plus all path-based MEP geometry. Compounding these, the written architectural scale ('1/4"=1'-0"') disagrees with the ~2.01x fit factor the geometry is actually plotted at, and the graphic bar prints '0 2 4 m' on an imperial set — a reviewer cannot scale off the sheet. The underlying SVG drafting is genuinely strong (this is a rendering/output-pipeline problem, not a drafting-quality problem), which makes these high-leverage: the correct data already exists and is being destroyed at the plot stage.
sequenced_actions: Move the line-legend, keynote, and key-plan blocks out of the scaled drawing group into sheet/title-block coordinate space in a reserved right-hand gutter (plan.py already has a _gutter path — extend it so the block is placed in sheet space, not nested inside the scale() transform), and run a collision test against the drawing bbox; drop residual opacity as a backstop.

Rewrite pdf_binder._parse_svg_drawing to honor graphics: read per-element stroke-width and emit '<w> w'; translate stroke-dasharray to '[..] 0 d'; honor each element's own fill instead of the hardcoded 0.8/0.9 grays; parse CSS <style> .lw-* classes; add a <path> handler (M/L/C/A). Strongly prefer delegating to resvg/cairosvg when available with the pure-Python path as fallback — this also recovers the dropped path-based MEP/structural geometry.

Fix scale honesty: emit an imperial graphic bar (0/4/8/16 ft) when units=='imperial' (sheets.py:82 currently hardcodes m/mm), and either plot at the true named scale (cap upscaling and pad the sheet) or recompute the title-block scale note from the actual fit factor so the written note matches the honest bar.

Make the binder consume SHEET_INDEX.json for page order and the cover list instead of sorted(glob('*.svg')): paginate in the curated Civil->Structural->Arch sequence and print 'sheet-no — TITLE' rows (e.g. 'C1.1 — SITE PLAN'), never internal '13 MEP-101_custom.svg' filenames; fall back to filename sort only when no index exists.
Add a dimension-tier declutter pass: stagger or suppress sub-minimum feature-tier segments that currently overprint at ~5-8px spacing with ~22px labels on A1.1, and break short segments to a leader.

Wrap note-block and PROJECT-field text to its box via textwrap (as the keynote legend already does) so nothing clips mid-word — the 'SCHAD Garage / ADU / Worksh…' PROJECT field is ellipsis-truncated on all 21 title blocks, and H2.2 program notes (CRC egress, septic re-check) lose their tails.
findings: BLOCKER: Opaque legend/keynote blocks overprint and occlude the drawing — _line_legend emits fill=#fff at drawing origin scaled with the model, burying South/North elevations, the section, and the lower-left floor plan; the A1.1 keynote box maps to 422x1664px in sheet space and overruns the sheet.

BLOCKER: PLOT_SET.pdf flattens all lineweights, dashes, and poche — pdf_binder reads 0 stroke-widths, emits no 'w' op, ignores dasharray, hardcodes fills, and has no <path> handler; verified 0 genuine linewidth ops and 0 dash ops in the PDF, and 31 <path> elements on S1-1 plus MEP path geometry are silently dropped.

HIGH: Scale integrity broken — title block prints '1/4"=1'-0"' but geometry is plotted at scale(2.0097) fit factor, and graphic_scale_bar always prints metric 'm/mm', so every imperial sheet shows '0 2 4 m' and the written note disagrees with the plotted scale.

MEDIUM: PDF page order ignores SHEET_INDEX.json (sorted glob gives ASCII order A,C,H,MEP,S… vs the curated Civil->Structural->Arch sequence) and the cover lists raw filenames ('13 MEP-101_custom.svg') instead of sheet-no + title.

MEDIUM: Feature-tier dimension strings overprint into illegible garble on A1.1 (11 sub-labels on one baseline at 5-8px spacing vs ~22px label width) — a real localized declutter bug (schedule/key-plan collision claims were false positives).
LOW: Note-block and PROJECT-field text clips mid-word — PROJECT reads 'SCHAD Garage / ADU / Worksh…' on all 21 title blocks and H2.2 program notes truncate mid-sentence instead of wrapping.

STRENGTH to preserve: the SVG source is professional — layered wall poche, dashed centerlines, multi-tier dimension strings with witness/tick marks, working keynote system, a true LINE LEGEND keyed to _TIER_WIDTHS, complete reviewer-recognizable title block with revisions table + north arrow + honesty stamp. The fix protects this quality through to the PDF.

workstreams.name: Discipline-sheet depth: symbol libraries, plan underlay, schedules, detail annotation
workstreams.rationale: Once real MEP geometry exists (Workstream 1), the discipline sheets need to draw it to an AE standard, and several architectural sheets need content depth. The MEP sheets are currently bubble-diagram overlays: every electrical device is an identical r=5 dot (differentiated only by a letter tag), every plumbing fixture is an identical teal square labeled with a literal '?' glyph, there are no home-runs/circuiting/riser diagrams, no routed piping, and no per-discipline legend. Discipline underlays reuse only a simplified building perimeter footprint rather than the richer 91-polygon/272-line architectural linework the plan already has. Schedules render internal wall GUIDs ('wal_ea06…') in a HOST ID column while room/level locators are already computed but unused, duplicate the CSI column on windows, and omit CD-standard door/frame/hardware/glazing/detail-ref and window U-factor/SHGC columns. This is 'real but shallow' — the sheets exist and carry substantive engineering annotation, so the work is depth and convention, not scaffolding.
sequenced_actions: Build a per-discipline symbol library and render device glyphs from the existing 'sym' field: distinct receptacle/GFCI/240V/switch/luminaire/panel/smoke-detector symbols on electrical, distinct fixture symbols (replacing the '?' placeholder squares) on plumbing; add a symbol legend block to every discipline sheet.

Draw routed runs from the now-modeled MEP geometry: home-run arcs with circuit tags to the panel on electrical, single-line supply/DWV/vent piping with slope arrows on plumbing, double-line sized ductwork on mechanical — plus connection/riser diagrams.

Reuse plan.py's full wall/room/door emitter as a grayed xref underlay beneath every discipline overlay (instead of the simplified perimeter footprint) so devices and runs sit on the real architectural background.

Rework schedules.py: replace the HOST ID GUID column with the room/grid location already computed in _annotate_csi (room/level/x/y/z are on every row but unrendered), drop the duplicate window CSI column, and add door/frame material+type, hardware group, glazing, head/jamb/sill detail-ref, and window operation + U-factor/SHGC columns; measure-fit column widths from the longest cell.

Drive detail annotation from assembly/wall-type data: add formal dimension strings with witness/extension lines between key faces (details currently carry material notes only inside text, no dimension strings), and increase leader density toward the AE norm of one leader per assembly layer.
Add material hatching (siding/batten/concrete) and opening articulation (muntins/divided lites, a grade line) to elevations, which currently use flat #d0d0d0 wall fill and plain window rectangles.
findings: BLOCKER (draw-half): MEP sheets are floor outlines + undifferentiated dots/squares + pasted note text, not routed system drawings — path count is 1 (building outline), all 38 electrical devices are identical circles, all plumbing fixtures are identical squares with '?' labels, the 34 lines are border grid ticks, and there is no legend or routed run anywhere (device sym IS shown as a letter tag, so 'discards the sym code' was overstated — the geometry is undifferentiated, not the type).

MEDIUM: Discipline sheets reuse only a simplified perimeter footprint, not the full architectural linework — S1-1 actually has rich foundation geometry (31 paths: slab edge + dashed footing/stem-wall) and S2-1/MEP-101 have footprint underlays, so the 'empty sheet / no geometry' framing was a false positive; the real gaps are missing legends, no circuiting/piping, '?' fixtures, and not reusing the plan's 91-polygon/272-line detail.

MEDIUM: Door/window schedules expose internal wall GUIDs (HOST ID = 'wal_ea06…') and omit CD-required columns — no hardware/frame/glazing/detail-ref on doors (fire rating '—' for all six), duplicated CSI column and no U-factor/SHGC on windows; room/level locators are already computed per row but rendered as the GUID instead.

LOW: Details and elevations are lighter than AE norm — details DO carry leadered material/fastener/flashing/membrane notes (the finding's 'unannotated lines' claim was false) but lack formal dimension strings; elevations carry datum tags + dimensioned storey heights but lack material hatching, muntins, and a grade line.

STRENGTH to build on: engineering content in the note blocks is real (NEC 220.82 ~43.2 kVA/180 A demand calc, DFU=10/WSFU=13.5 with 3/4" service, q=1040 psf <= 1500 bearing check), and coverage is broad and correctly numbered (A/C/S/A4/S4/MEP-101..301/H) with a consistent SHEET_INDEX.json.

workstreams.name: Presentation-grade rendering: textured PBR, glass/light material extensions, AO, hero image
workstreams.rationale: The renderings leg has a genuinely strong live viewer (ACES tone mapping, PMREM IBL, 4-source rig, PCF soft shadows, UnrealBloom, section cut, measure tool, element-aware inspect cards from glTF extras) but stops short of AE hero-render quality, and it ships no portable/baked output. Surfaces are flat solid colors: the glTF has zero textures and zero UVs (every primitive is POSITION+NORMAL only), so concrete/drywall/stucco/wood are indistinguishable pastels. There are no glTF material extensions — glass is fake alpha (no KHR_materials_transmission/ior/volume), luminaires have no emissiveFactor, and there is no KHR_lights_punctual, so the file opened in Blender/model-viewer/Windows 3D Viewer is dead-flat with no lights. IBL comes from a 1280x720 LDR JPEG so metals reflect a clamped sky. There is no ambient-occlusion pass (composer is only RenderPass+bloom), and the pack contains no baked hero raster anywhere — a user needing a picture for a cover sheet or proposal must screenshot the WebGL app. The lit scene and 55-key PBR palette already exist, so these are additive fidelity upgrades that directly move the 'AE-quality rendering' needle.
sequenced_actions: Generate planar/triplanar UVs during tessellation (write TEXCOORD_0 + TANGENT accessors at mesh.py:2091/2142) and add 3-4 tiling PBR texture sets (concrete, drywall, metal, wood) keyed to material names, referenced via glTF images/textures/samplers + baseColorTexture/normalTexture/roughnessTexture — the single biggest read upgrade.

Declare extensionsUsed and emit material extensions: KHR_materials_transmission+ior(~1.5)+volume for the window key (replacing the alpha-blend fake glass), emissiveFactor (+KHR_materials_emissive_strength) for luminaire/indicator keys, and a KHR_lights_punctual rig mirroring the viewer's key/fill/rim so external tools render correctly.

Add a screen-space AO pass (GTAO/N8AO) to the composer between RenderPass and bloom, exposed behind the existing 'Soft shadows' toggle or a new checkbox — note the bundled three build exports no AO pass, so add it to three_bundle.js first; add per-object contact shadows so equipment grounds to the slab.

Ship an HDR studio environment (.hdr via RGBELoader or a pre-encoded RGBE data URI) and feed PMREM from it instead of the 1280x720 LDR JPEG, keeping the JPEG only as the visible backdrop, so metals pick up crisp high-dynamic-range highlights.

Add a headless offscreen render step (playwright/puppeteer driving the existing viewer at the iso/end/top camera presets, or server-side GL) that writes hero_iso.png + 2-3 vignettes into the pack and embeds them in the index.html and the PDF cover sheet.

Cleanup: when el_meta is non-empty, skip appending the 10 material-aggregate meshes/nodes and the aggregate index bufferView (10% dead index bytes + 10 phantom objects in external tools), keeping them only for the empty-element fallback; raise radial segment counts for round equipment/pipes for presentation exports (headroom is large at 4.5K tris).
findings: HIGH: glTF carries zero texture maps or UVs — all 119 primitives are POSITION+NORMAL only, all 10 materials are baseColorFactor-only, no images/textures/samplers; concrete/drywall/stucco render as flat pastels (the single biggest thing separating this from a client render).

HIGH: No glTF material extensions — no extensionsUsed; window is plain alpha-blend (no transmission/ior/volume), no material has emissiveFactor, no KHR_lights_punctual, so the file in Blender/model-viewer/Windows 3D Viewer has no lights and dead-flat glass.

MEDIUM: No ambient occlusion or contact shadows in the viewer — composer is only RenderPass+UnrealBloom (no SSAO/GTAO/N8AO), one shadow-casting directional light, single ground plane; corners and seated equipment read flat/floaty.

MEDIUM: Environment/IBL is a 1280x720 LDR JPEG fed to PMREM as scene.environment — metals (copper 0.85, roof 0.55, beams 0.8) reflect a clamped [0,1] sky, so highlights are dull and surfaces read plastic.

MEDIUM: Deliverable contains no baked hero render — no *.png/*.jpg building image outside assets/, PLOT_SET.pdf has 0 embedded raster images, index.html embeds none; the only 3D output is the live WebGL viewer, so any needed picture must be screenshotted by hand.

LOW: 10 material-aggregate meshes + the aggregate index bufferView are dead weight in every glTF — nodes 0-9 are never referenced by the scene (only the empty-element fallback), wasting a ~10% index buffer and showing 10 phantom objects in external tools.

LOW: Whole model is 4,488 triangles so round equipment silhouettes are faceted and the viewer raises the pipe/conduit crease threshold to 40deg to hide facet edges (partial — the Schad pack actually contains no pipe/conduit meshes and the cylinder default was already raised 14->32, so this is minor for this artifact).

STRENGTH to build on: the viewer is already a real presentation renderer (ACES + sRGB, PMREM IBL, 4-light rig, 2048px PCF soft shadows, bloom, section cut, measure tool, iso/top/end presets), materials are a semantically differentiated 55-key PBR palette with walls forced opaque, per-element extras drive element-aware inspect cards + category/level isolation, and delivery is fully offline (vendored three.js r160, base64 glTF, inlined assets, boot-error banner).

top_do_now: Wire projects/schad/build_llmbim.py + schad_mep.py to actually call the routing/sizing engine (mep_autoroute / place_pipe / place_duct / place_conduit / place_fitting) for DWV, domestic water, radiant PEX, sprinkler, and feeder runs — this alone converts 'FULL MEP = zero' into populated takeoffs, first-class IFC MEP entities, and real geometry for the MEP sheets to draw. Highest single-lever action.

Move the LINE LEGEND / KEYNOTES / KEY PLAN blocks out of scaled drawing space into a reserved sheet-space gutter with a collision test, so the plan, both elevations, and the section stop being buried under opaque white boxes (currently an immediate AE rejection on the three primary architectural sheets).

Rewrite pdf_binder to preserve graphics — emit per-element stroke-width 'w', translate stroke-dasharray to dashes, honor each element's fill, and add a <path> handler (or delegate to resvg/cairosvg) — restoring the HEAVY/MEDIUM/LIGHT/DASHED hierarchy and poche the SVGs already compute and recovering the silently-dropped path geometry.

Fix scale honesty: emit an imperial graphic bar (0/4/8/16 ft) instead of the hardcoded metric 'm', and reconcile the title-block scale note with the actual fit factor so a reviewer can scale off the sheet and the written scale is not false.

Add textured PBR to the glTF (write TEXCOORD_0/TANGENT UVs + 3-4 tiling texture sets for concrete/drywall/metal/wood) and material extensions (KHR_materials_transmission glass, emissive luminaires, baked KHR_lights_punctual) — the biggest jump from 'CAD look' toward a client-presentation hero render, using the lit scene and 55-key palette that already exist.

Replace the door/window schedule HOST ID GUID column with the room/level location already computed in _annotate_csi, drop the duplicate window CSI column, and add the missing CD columns (door/frame/hardware/glazing/detail-ref; window U-factor/SHGC) — a low-effort credibility win on a sheet reviewers scrutinize.
